summary:
The use of biomolecular methods such as microarrays for identifying allergenic pollen lacks scalability and affordability for widespread aerobiological monitoring due to high costs and reliance on specialized equipment. These tools are currently limited to clinical diagnostic environments rather than feasible applications in field-level air quality or public health surveillance systems.  

\boxed{B} The correct choice reflects this limitation—biomolecular techniques are impractical for routine outdoor environmental polling compared with traditional approaches.
